summary: Completed comprehensive frontend testing of P0 features for Simple Football mode. All 11 features tested and verified working correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: NewGame.jsx back button correctly navigates to /dashboard for all sports (verified via code review - lines 241-248)
SimpleFootballLiveGame.jsx keyboard shortcuts properly implemented with input field exclusion (lines 546-572)
FootballLiveGame.jsx (Advanced mode) keyboard shortcuts working correctly (lines 584-655)
Quarter transition dialogs properly show halftime options after Q2 and final/overtime options after Q4
Enhanced turnover workflows correctly ask for thrower then interceptor (interception) and fumbler then recoverer from either team (fumble)
seed_data_creation: Demo mode uses static initial data (Central Wolves vs Riverside Panthers)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All P0 features verified working. Simple Football demo at /demo/football/simple, Advanced Football demo at /demo/football. NewGame page requires auth but back button code verified via code review.

back_button_navigation.notes: NewGame.jsx lines 241-248 correctly navigate to /dashboard for all sports. Requires auth to test via UI.

scoring_buttons_styling.notes: TD, XP, 2PT, FG, Safety buttons have bg-zinc-700 (dark grey) with border-2 border-white (white outline) and text-3xl font-black (larger text)

clock_controls.notes: Clock controls show -1m, -1s, +1s, +1m buttons instead of ±10 seconds

spacebar_keyboard_shortcut_simple.notes: Spacebar toggles clock running state in Simple Football mode

backslash_keyboard_shortcut_simple.notes: Backslash toggles clock running state in Simple Football mode

spacebar_keyboard_shortcut_advanced.notes: Spacebar toggles clock running state in Advanced Football mode

backslash_keyboard_shortcut_advanced.notes: Backslash toggles clock running state in Advanced Football mode

q2_halftime_dialog.notes: Advancing from Q2 shows dialog with 'Go to Halftime' and 'Start 3rd Quarter' options

q4_final_overtime_dialog.notes: Advancing from Q4 shows dialog with 'End Game (Final)' and 'Start 1st Overtime' options

steps: Step 2: 'Who threw the interception? (offense team)' - asks for thrower
Step 3: 'Who made the interception? (defense team)' - asks for interceptor
Step 2: 'Who fumbled? (offense team)' - asks for fumbler
Step 3: 'Who recovered the fumble?' - shows both offense (green) and defense (red with TURNOVER label) options

mocked_api_note: DEMO MODE - Frontend-only demo with no backend API calls. Stats are not persisted.
